events.title: (Re)sounding: Art Exhibition
events.description: Sound is not simply something we hear, but something we inhabit, shape, and are shaped by. Through immersive audio installations, evocative visual translations of sound, and interactive artworks that invite participation, the artists in (Re)sounding explore sound as a dynamic subject and material. This exhibition is an invitation to listen more deeply—to feel the weight and resonance of sound, to dwell inside it, and to hear the world in new ways.

Top three male and female finishers will be recognized on the podium.

events.title: Jylian Gustlin + Lola: Exhibition
events.description:
Gallery MAR is pleased to present a summer exhibition featuring new work by Lola and Jylian Gustlin, opening **July 3rd** with an artist reception from **6–9 pm** at the gallery’s Main Street location in Park City. On view for **three weeks**, the exhibition brings together two distinctive contemporary artists whose practices explore abstraction through color, material, and perception.

Lola, a self-taught visual artist based in the San Francisco Bay Area, is best known for her intensely vibrant abstractions created with tinted epoxy resin. Working on a raised horizontal surface, she allows the resin to flow, pool, and spill over the edges of the panel, creating luminous compositions framed by gravity-defying drips that have become a signature of her work. Her newest series, *Quiet Places*, marks an evolution in her practice—moving away from densely layered forms toward simpler shapes, softer palettes, and shadowy overlays that invite viewers to experience moments of calm and serenity. For Lola, color remains the central language of emotion, and each work is intended to create an experience that allows viewers to find their own meaning and sense of optimism within the composition.

In conversation with Lola’s emotive abstractions are the richly layered paintings of Jylian Gustlin. Influenced by the San Francisco Bay Area Figurative painters and informed by her background in computer science and digital graphics programming, Gustlin’s work explores the intersection of art, mathematics, and perception. Drawing on concepts such as the Fibonacci sequence and entropy, she creates complex compositions using oil, acrylic, charcoal, wax, gold leaf, pastel, and graphite. Gustlin builds her surfaces through drawing, painting, and scratching into the material itself, resulting in paintings that feel almost sculptural—dynamic fields of gesture, symbol, and color that encourage viewers to look closely and discover new relationships within the work.

Together, Lola and Gustlin...
events.location: Gallery MAR, 436 Main St, Park City, UT, 84060

events.title: Cacao Workshop
events.description:
**Cacao Workshop**

Presented by: Esmeralda Torres and the Lopez Aquino Family of Chiapas, Mexico, and Utah Experience a cultural immersion into the Zoque-Maya chocolate-making tradition! Participants will learn the origin and cultural significance of chocolate, use traditional tools to grind cacao beans from farms in Chiapas into chocolate using hands-on processes, and taste traditional chocolate beverages, chocolates, and other recipes. This workshop offers space to reflect on the profound depth of ancestral knowledge and earth-sustaining practices, while examining the vibrant parallels within Utah's diverse communities, where generational wisdom is honored preserving ancestral recipes and traditional food preparation methods. 

Class includes all materials and ingredients. Ages 12 and up. $30. Scholarships available. Please email Team@summitcommunitygardens.org for more information. 

**Taller de Cacao**

Presentado por: Esmeralda Torres y la familia López Aquino, de Chiapas (México) y Utah ¡Viva una inmersión cultural en la tradición chocolatera zoque-maya! Los participantes aprenderán sobre el origen y el significado cultural del chocolate, utilizarán herramientas tradicionales para moler granos de cacao —provenientes de las fincas en Chiapas— y transformarlos en chocolate mediante procesos prácticos, además de degustar bebidas de chocolate tradicionales, chocolates y otras recetas. Este taller ofrece un espacio para reflexionar sobre la profunda riqueza del conocimiento ancestral y las prácticas de sostenibilidad ecológica, al tiempo que examina los vibrantes paralelismos existentes en las diversas comunidades de Utah, donde se honra la sabiduría generacional mediante la preservación de recetas ancestrales y métodos tradicionales de preparación de alimentos.

La clase incluye todos los materiales e ingredientes. A partir de los 12 años. $30. Becas disponibles. Escriba a Team@summitcommunitygardens.org para más información....

events.title: Kenneth Peloke: Year of the Horse
events.description:
Working in oil on large-format canvases, Kenneth Peloke approaches the horse not as subject but as a vessel for energy, instinct, and the kind of presence that resists easy translation into paint. His process is physical and deliberate, built through layers of mark-making that accumulate into something that feels less rendered than conjured. 

Year of the Horse brings together new works that explore what the animal has always represented in the human imagination: freedom, strength, the untamed thing we are drawn to and can never fully possess. 

Join us July 18th to experience new works by Kenneth Peloke at Gallery MAR, Park City....

events.title: Michael Kessler: All Resin
events.description:
Michael Kessler has spent decades exploring the boundary between gesture and geometry, and his resin works bring that inquiry into sharper focus than ever.

Each painting is built from up to fifty micro-thin translucent layers, fused and accumulated until something luminous emerges. Biomorphic forms weave through geometric structures, nature's logic made visible through an almost meditative process of application and restraint. Kessler likens his gestural freedom to a kind of painterly tai chi, controlled, but never rigid.

In his all-resin works, that layered depth takes on new dimension. Light travels through the surface rather than simply reflecting off it, making each piece feel as much like a living object as a painting.

Join us August 7th to experience new works by Michael Kessler at Gallery MAR, Park City....
events.location: Gallery MAR, 436 Main St, Park City, UT, 84060

events.title: Recess on Main - Wine Bingo with Italian Wines
events.description:
Get ready for a mental and flavor challenge with Professor Laura Wheatley while exploring Italy’s iconic regions and varietals. events.title: Matt Flint: Sleeping in the Forest
events.description:
Matt Flint doesn't begin with a plan. No sketches, no predetermined composition, just canvas, paint, and a process as intuitive as the landscapes that shaped him.

Raised in rural Missouri and now living in Wyoming's Wind River Mountains, Flint has spent a lifetime in close study of the natural world, its textures, its rhythms, its constant state of becoming. That attention finds its way into every surface he paints. His works build through layers of creation and destruction, each image emerging as another recedes, holding the tension between what was and what might come next.

The result is painting that moves the way wilderness does, familiar and elusive at once, inviting you closer while always suggesting there's something just beyond the next ridge.

Join us September 5th to experience new works by Matt Flint at Gallery MAR, Park City....

At 9 a.m. all volunteers head to their Serve Park City service projects they have signed up for on JustServe.org/ServeParkCity that are being held on 9/19/26. (These projects will be posted by August 1, 2026) Last year we had 35 different projects all over the Park City area that ran from 9 a.m. -12 noon with over 1200 volunteers participating. We invite all to serve. 

This event is hosted by Serve Park City, Inc. A nonprofit whose mission is to build community through service. Each year we partner with over 30 nonprofits and organizations in hosting a day of service, but find opportunities to serve throughout the year on JustServe.org/ServeParkCity. events.title: Miles Glynn: Westernish
events.description:
Join us October 23rd to kick off our first show of the fall, and experience new works by Miles Glynn at Gallery MAR, Park City.

Miles Glynn grew up seeing the American West through a car window, the son of an Army photojournalist, trained from childhood to see the world in frames and compositions. That eye never left him.

Working from Marfa, Texas, Glynn builds layered mixed-media paintings from vintage photographs, forgotten advertising, and Western ephemera, combining silkscreen, paint, and collage into works that feel at once deeply familiar and entirely new. The textures of deteriorating buildings, weathered billboards, and desert landscapes find their way into every surface,  documents of time, resilience, and the stories that linger after everything else fades.

His work doesn't ask you to see what he sees. It asks you to find what only you would....

events.title: Allen Stone
events.description:
Grammy-nominated artist Allen Stone delivers an unforgettable fusion of soul, funk, and feel-good energy. Allen Stone, a soul/R&B luminary, has enraptured global audiences with his electrifying performances and soulful melodies, embodying the essence of classic soul legends. 
His genre-defying style, blending funk, gospel, folk-rock, and soul, has garnered critical acclaim and a devoted following. Stone's journey to musical stardom commenced in Chewelah, Washington, where his upbringing immersed him in gospel music as a pastor's son. Relocating to Seattle, he tirelessly toured the West Coast in his '87 Buick, swiftly gaining acclaim for his dynamic live shows, often playing up to 200 dates annually alongside luminaries such as Stevie Wonder, Al Green, Dave Matthews Band, Erykah Badu, and Gary Clark Jr. Notably, Stone recently hit the road with Chris Stapleton, further solidifying his status as a tour de force in the music industry.
Alongside his prolific touring schedule, Stone has graced national television screens and mentored on ABC's American Idol, underscoring his esteemed position in the music realm. Stone continues to garner critical acclaim with his latest body of work, Mystery, featuring the title track in collaboration with PJ Morton. This release brings infectious rhythms and emotional depth, solidifying Stone’s place as a powerful voice in modern soul music. 
Opening support Wyatt Pike is an indie-folk songwriter known for crafting story-driven songs filled with vivid imagery, emotional honesty, and reflections on real-life experiences. He first gained attention with his debut EP, Brand New Boy, and most recently released the acoustic project Face The Weather. His music explores the journey of personal growth and self-discovery. As Alt Recess noted, his work represents "the next chapter—more assured, more grounded, but still brimming with the tender honesty that put him on the map." His upcoming 2026 releases will continue to build on that authentic and

events.title: Better Than Ezra with Special Guests Vertical Horizon and TONIC
events.description:
Get ready for a night of iconic ’90s and early-2000s alternative rock as Better Than Ezra and Vertical Horizon take the stage together for an unforgettable live show.
Better Than Ezra, known for their chart-topping hits like “Good,” “Desperately Wanting,” and “King of New Orleans,” rose to prominence during the alternative rock boom of the 1990s. With their melodic hooks, thoughtful lyrics, and energetic live performances, the band has remained a fan favorite for decades.
Joining them is Vertical Horizon, the multi-platinum band behind massive radio hits “Everything You Want,” “You’re a God,” and “Best I Ever Had.” Their emotionally driven songwriting and polished rock sound helped define an era of modern alternative and continue to resonate with audiences today.
Together, these two beloved bands deliver a hit-packed night of nostalgia, sing-along anthems, and timeless alternative rock. Whether you grew up with these songs or discovered them later, this show promises a high-energy, feel-good concert experience you won’t want to miss.
Music has an undeniable way of bringing people together, creating connections that run as deep as family. That bond is at the heart of Tonic, the GRAMMY® Award–nominated, multi-platinum rock trio made up of Emerson Hart (vocals, guitar), Jeff Russo (lead guitar), and Dan Lavery (bass).
Since forming in the mid-1990s, Tonic has built a lasting legacy through eloquent songwriting, lyrical honesty, and unforgettable melodies. Their debut album Lemon Parade went platinum and produced the iconic “If You Could Only See,” the #1 most-played rock song of 1997. The band went on to score six Top 10 singles, sell over 4 million records, and earn two GRAMMY® Award nominations, including for “Take Me As I Am.”
More than two decades later, Tonic’s music continues to resonate across generations. Fans—young and old—still sing every word, proving the songs are bigger than time and bigger than the band itself. After brief breaks and successful solo caree

events.title: Boney James
events.description:
Saxophonist, Producer and Composer Boney James’  New Album ‘Slow Burn’
slow burn: noun, informal
A feeling that grows with a slow but deliberate intensity.
For saxophonist and composer Boney James, these words perfectly describe the music he has  crafted for his new Concord Records album, Slow Burn, to be released on October 18, 2024.
“When I mentioned that phrase to people as a possible title, they said, “That also refers to your  whole career in a way” James says with a laugh. “This is my 19th record and I feel like I’m just  hitting my stride.”
Slow Burn is a new high point in a remarkable career that now spans more than three decades  and includes a long list of accolades; four GRAMMY nominations, three NAACP Image Award  nominations, a Soul Train Award for Best Jazz Album and four RIAA Gold Record certifications.  In 2009, Billboard named James one of the Top 3 Contemporary Jazz Artists of the decade. In  2024 year Boney became the first ever artist to score 20 number one singles on the Billboard Smooth Jazz chart.
But Boney James has never been one to look backwards and Slow Burn, which the artist  produced, marks several firsts. Beginning with the album’s opening track, ‘Arcadia.’ The very first notes on the album are the acoustic bass playing of the legendary Marcus Miller.  It’s one of the few times that Miller, known for his electric bass guitar work, has committed his  standup bass playing to a recording and the first time that James has incorporated an upright bass  into his own music
Next is James’ intriguing interpretation of the classic ‘Butterfly.’ Co-written by master  keyboardist Herbie Hancock and Bennie Maupin, the track features Marcus Miller again as well  as young lion Cory Henry’s inspired electric piano. “This beautiful melody got stuck in my head”  explains James. “I have never covered a tune by a legend like Herbie before and I hope we did  him justice.”
The title track, ‘Slow Burn,’ is one of four tunes on the album that James co-wrote an

events.title: Dylan Scott
events.description: ABOUT DYLAN SCOTT:Despite a multi-Platinum story and six No. 1 singles, including two fresh smashes in 2025 alone, Curb Records hitmaker Dylan Scott has always kept an underdog mindset. A rowdy roughneck with a romantic side – one whose modern edge was sharpened by timeless, emotional twang – the consummate family man broke out with the epic triple-Platinum devotion of “My Girl” in 2016 and followed up with scrappy, under-the-radar consistency. Chart-topping heart-forward anthems like “Hooked,” “Nobody” and “New Truck” all reached RIAA-certified status, but last year launched the crooner into the stratosphere. Posting back-to-back No. 1s with Gold-certified “Boys Back Home” (with Dylan Marlowe) and Platinum-certified “This Town’s Been Too Good to Us,” Scott wrapped 2025 as the third most-played country artist of 2025 and continues his hot streak with his current single, Platinum-certified “What He’ll Never Have,” which nears the Top 10 at Country radio and was honored by ASCAP as one of the most-performed country songs of 2025. With nearly 5 billion career streams, legions of fans and countless sold-out shows to his credit, his stardom has somehow grown in relative stealth. But with his third full album, Easy Does It, he’s not sweating. Sure of himself, his talent and his fans, Scott heads toward a new creative chapter with total confidence. Featuring 11 tracks, his signature vocal and vivid intensity take center stage on a set defined by personal truth – whether through heartbreak, romantic devotion or small-town dedication. The in-demand live act spent last year on his Country Till I Die Tour, headlining his biggest venues yet including his first-ever arenas and record-breaking attendance, and his Easy Does It Tour. He will continue headlining across the country on his Till I Can’t I Will Tour, launching this spring.

events.title: Geoffrey Gersten: New Works
events.description:
Geoffrey Gersten's large-scale oil paintings turn the forgotten objects of mid-20th-century life into something unmistakably alive, join us November 27th for his Gallery MAR debut.

We're proud to welcome Geoffrey Gersten to Gallery MAR for his first show with us. His studio smells like oil paint and nostalgia. Matchbooks, bottle caps, vintage toys, and torn advertising ephemera line every surface, remnants of a mid-20th-century world that most people have forgotten and Gersten refuses to let go.

Working in oil on large-format canvases, he elevates these once-ordinary objects to something close to monumental, rendering them with a precision that borders on photorealistic. Step forward and the paint asserts itself as unmistakably made by hand.

It's a tightrope Gersten walks with confidence: classical technique in service of deeply personal subject matter. “New Works” brings together his latest paintings, each one a celebration of life's timeless pleasures and treasures, rendered with an ever-fresh spirit that makes the past feel startlingly alive.

Join us November 27th from 6–9 PM to experience new works by Geoffrey Gersten at Gallery MAR, Park City....
events.location: Gallery MAR, 436 Main St, Park City, UT, 84060

events.title: Maoli
events.description:
Now breaking into the mainstream with a one-of-a-kind blend of island reggae and country soul, Hawaiian-born rising star Maoli is bringing his people—and his sound—to the world like never before.
The golden-voiced native of upcountry Maui has released nine independent albums, earned over half a billion global streams, and spent nearly 15 years touring and recording, becoming a major fixture across the Pacific Islands and beyond. Inspired by traditional Hawaiian music (the birthplace of the steel guitar), reggae, rock, and country legends like Dolly Parton, Willie Nelson, and George Strait, Maoli has built a signature style that connects Polynesia to the American West Coast—and all the way to Texas.
With a growing global fanbase, Maoli’s Hawaiian-country sound has fueled viral hits and powerful covers, including Brett Young’s “Mercy” (18+ million Spotify streams) and “In Case You Didn’t Know” (10+ million Spotify streams). Now teaming up with hit producers Dann and David Huff, Maoli continues bridging the gap between island soul and country tradition with his latest release, Last Sip of Summer (Island Vibe Edition)—out now.

events.description:
All of Summit County is invited to join Shelley Read for a discussion of her book, *Go as a River*, which is our community's One Book, One Community book this year. 

*Go as a River* is available with no-wait as an ebook and eaudiobook on Libby. Physical copies are available in both the Park City and Summit County Library and at Dolly’s Bookstore.

events.title: Park City Film Presents: Power Ballad
events.description:
When Rick (Paul Rudd), a past-his-prime wedding singer, meets fading boy-band star Danny (Nick Jonas) during a gig, the two bond over music and a late-night jam session. But when Danny turns one of Rick’s songs into the hit that reignites his career, Rick sets out to reclaim the recognition he believes he deserves. From writer-director John Carney (SING STREET, ONCE), “Power Ballad” is a feel-good story about music, self-respect, friendship, and the price of ambition.

$8 for general admission, $7 for students and seniors, and $6 for members of Park City Film.

**SHOW TIMES: (July 10-12)** Fri/Sat: 7:00 PM Sun: 6:00 PM...

events.title: Rick Springfield
events.description:
RICK SPRINGFIELD BIO: 
Over the past four decades, Rick Springfield has worn many hats as an entertainer and performer. The creator of some of the finest power-pop of the ’80s, a Grammy winning singer, songwriter, and musician who has sold 25 million albums and scored 17 U.S. Top 40 hits, including “Jessie’s Girl,” “Don’t Talk to Strangers,” “An Affair of the Heart,” "I've Done Everything for You,” “Love Somebody,” and “Human Touch.” He’s an accomplished actor who has starred opposite Meryl Streep in the feature film “Ricki and the Flash,” gave a chameleonic performance as the creepy Dr. Irving Pitlor in HBO’s prestige drama “True Detective,” earned great reviews for his portrayal of Lucifer on the CW hit “Supernatural” and most recently played the insane Pastor Charles on FX’s worldwide hit series “American Horror Story.” An author as well, both his candid 2010 memoir Late, Late at Night (which Rolling Stone named one of the 25 greatest rock memoirs of all time) and his 2014 comedic novel Magnificent Vibration earned rave reviews and spots on the New York Times Best Sellers’ list. In 2014, Springfield was honored with a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, located around the corner from the first apartment he lived in when he first arrived in the U.S. from Australia in 1971. Springfield’s Sirius-XM radio show, Working Class DJ, is a huge hit and airs weekly on 80s on 8. His new album “Automatic” is out now. Automatic is his first album of all new original material in 5 years. 
Rick Springfield Premium Ticket & Merch Package
. Premium Concert Ticket (or GA where applicable). Exclusive T-Shirt 
. Exclusive Merchandise Pack
Within 14 days of the show you will receive an email from ONELIVE with further details regarding your package fulfillment and requesting your T-shirt size. Merchandise is shipped to you the week of the show and may arrive shortly before or after your show date. Merchandise subject to change. For questions related to your Premium Ticket & Merch packa

events.title: UB40 featuring Ali Campbell
events.description:
UB40 Featuring Ali Campbell – Live in Concert
Get ready for a legendary night of reggae under the stars as UB40 featuring Ali Campbell brings their iconic sound to Concerts on the Slopes in Park City, where Music Meets the Mountains.
With Ali Campbell’s unmistakable voice leading the way, UB40 has delivered some of the most beloved songs of all time, including Red Red Wine, Can’t Help Falling in Love, Kingston Town, Here I Am (Come and Take Me), and Food for Thought. Their music has defined generations, blending reggae rhythms with pop sensibility and global appeal.
Set against the stunning backdrop of Canyons Village at Park City Mountain, Concerts on the Slopes offers a one-of-a-kind outdoor concert experience. Enjoy cool mountain temperatures, sweeping alpine views, and an energetic yet intimate setting with 5,000 seats and top-tier sound and production.
Make it a full Park City experience—arrive early, explore the village, and settle in for an unforgettable summer evening filled with timeless music, mountain air, and good vibes.

Crowd-free merchandise zone

events.title: Walk to End Alzheimer's Summit/Wasatch County
events.description:
Held annually in more than 600 communities nationwide, the Alzheimer's Association Walk to End Alzheimer's® is the world's largest fundraiser for Alzheimer's care, support and research. This inspiring event calls on participants of all ages and abilities to join the fight against the disease.

Since 1989, the Alzheimer’s Association mobilized millions of Americans in the Alzheimer's Association Memory Walk® ; now the Alzheimer's Association is continuing to lead the way with Walk to End Alzheimer's.

While there is no fee to register for Walk, all participants are encouraged to raise critical funds that allow the Alzheimer’s Association to provide 24/7 care and support and advance research toward methods of prevention, treatment and, ultimately, a cure....

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description:
Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date.

A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere.

Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Gorgeous Gourds
events.description:
Psychedelic Funk band Gorgeous Gourds combine smooth harmonies, deep tasty grooves, and original songwriting.

The Gorgeous Gourd's just released album \(2/22/22\) "Peace and Love" reminds us why we go see and hear live music. The band has evolved to a rock solid synergistic group of accomplished musicians. Their powerful singing and songwriting is evident on their new CD, with 14 worthy cuts combining an eclectic combination of psychedelic funk, folk, tasty jam grooves, smooth harmonies and soulful ballads. Fred, Lucas, and Sarah all share the artful original songwriting. Iconic KRCL DJ John Florence commented on the new album, "Not a stinker in the bunch!”

Formed in Salt Lake City, Utah in 2015, founding members are Fred Hayes \(bass, vocals\), and the strong and steady Bob Smith \(drums\). Lucas Jones \(lead guitar,vocals\) provides sublime and powerful guitar licks and vocals, together with the guitar of Jacob Dahlberg and the dazzling vocals of Heather McCartin. 

Whether you enjoy easing into a bluesy flow ride, or getting all the kinks out on the dance floor, the Gorgeous Gourds is the party you'll keep coming back to. The title of their new album "Peace and Love" is a message they spread with unbridled energy and enthusiasm.

Like they say, "You're Gorgeous, we're the Gourds. Together we are Gorgeous Gourds."

GorgeousGourds.net...

events.title: Eric Heideman
events.description:
Eric Heideman is a 27-year-old blues musician living in Austin, Texas, from Salt Lake City, Utah.

Heideman was personally requested to open shows for ZZ Top at Eccles Theatre and Walter Trout and Mike Zito at The State Room. He was a featured performer at the Utah Blues Fest in 2022, 2023, & 2024. 

He was a featured performer at SXSW 2025 and the 2025 Durango Blues Train.

He recorded his latest album, “Third Degree Gravity” with Grammy Nominated Victor Wainwright and International Blues Challenge winner JW-Jones.

events.title: Queen Nation - Tribute to the Music of Queen at  Forum Fest At Canyons VIllage
events.description: Queen Nation, a tribute to Queen, was formed in 2004. Queen Nation consists of: Gregory Finsley on vocals & keyboards as Freddie Mercury, Mike McManus on guitar as Brian May, Pete Burke on drums as Roger Taylor, and Parker Combs on bass as John Deacon. The mission of the show is to carry on the musical torch and pay homage to the golden age of vintage Queen concerts. Queen Nation’s live 90-minute production of Queen’s greatest hits preserves the image, sound, and stage persona of vintage Queen. Audiences are amazed at the accuracy of Queen Nation’s live retrospective journey through such Queen songs as We Will Rock You – Bohemian Rhapsody – We Are The Champions – Crazy Little Thing Called Love – You’re My Best Friend – Another One Bites The Dust – Under Pressure and many more. They have performed in front of thousands of people at casinos, fairs, festivals and private affairs. For more info visit www.queennation.com....
events.location: Forum Fest At Canyons VIllage, 4000 Canyon Resort Drive, Park City

events.title: Opera Around the World: Great Britain — Park City Opera
events.description:
Paris is calling - and you don't need a plane ticket! Romantic, shimmering, and utterly enchanting, this evening features excerpts  from PCO's upcoming performances of Roméo et Juliette https://www.parkcityopera.org/romeo-et-juliette - because nobody does passion quite like the French.

Set against Park City's stunning mountain backdrop, we invite you to bring a picnic, gather your loved ones \(dogs included\), and enjoy opera and vocal chamber music in an open-air, relaxed community setting. This event is free and no tickets are required.

events.description:
To kick off Park City Opera's "Opera Around the World" series, your passport gets its first stamp right here at home. Featuring singers visiting from around the country to perform in PCO's The Tender Land https://www.parkcityopera.org/the-tender-land , this series opener celebrates the breadth and diversity of American music - and keeps the July 4th celebrations going an extra week!

Set against Park City's stunning mountain backdrop, we invite you to bring a picnic, gather your loved ones \(dogs included\), and enjoy opera and vocal chamber music in an open-air, relaxed community setting. This event is free and no tickets are required....

events.description: Alicia Stockman is a Utah-based folk-meets-Americana singer-songwriter whose music pulls back the veil to reveal everyday vulnerability. Influenced by the vivid storytelling of powerhouse songwriters like Patty Griffin, Indigo Girls, and Jewel, she finds her home in the folk and Americana scene.

From July 2 to 4, the free multi-day celebration brings expanded hours, three themed concert nights, nightly drone shows and fireworks, the Freedom 5K, morning yoga, silent discos, an educational hawk show, the Nuzzles & Co. events.title: "Horizons" New Paintings by Luke Anderson
events.description:
In his latest body of work, Luke Anderson explores the enduring character of the American West through a contemporary lens. Balancing sweeping landscapes with intimate moments of ranch life, Anderson's paintings capture the rugged beauty, quiet resilience, and timeless spirit of the places and people that define the region. Rich in atmosphere and authenticity, these works invite viewers to experience the West not as a relic of the past, but as a living and evolving landscape.

Join us for the opening reception on **Friday, June 26, 6-8 pm**. Guests will meet the artist and enjoy a live music and refreshments....
events.location: Meyer Gallery, Park City, UT

events.title: "Tucked In" New Paintings by Colby Sanford
events.description:
*Tucked In* is a new collection of paintings by Colby Sanford that celebrates the quiet beauty of home, family, and the landscapes that shape our daily lives. Through luminous light, rich color, and thoughtful composition, Sanford captures moments of stillness and belonging, inviting viewers to reflect on the places and relationships that ground us. These works offer a contemporary perspective on the enduring comforts of connection, memory, and place.

The opening reception will be held on **Friday, June 26, 6-8 pm**. Guests will meet the artist and enjoy live music and refreshments.
events.location: Meyer Gallery, Park City, UT

Put on your walking shoes and explore Park City’s Historic Main Street! Explore the architecture and stories of Park City’s unique history-from the days as a booming mine town to an emerging ski town. The Park City Museum’s popular Historic Main Street Public Walking Tours are Monday through Friday at 2 p.m. from June 29 to September 19. Meet at the Museum store. Cost is $15 per person. 

No tours on August 8 \(Kimball Arts Festival\) and September 7 \(Labor Day/Miners’ Day\). 

The tour is most appropriate for ages 13 and up. Please arrive 10 minutes early, wear comfortable walking shoes, bring water, and wear sun protection. The tour lasts about one hour and fifteen minutes. If you have general questions about the tour or want to schedule a private tour for twelve or more people, please contact Diane at 435-574-9554....

events.title: Glenwood Cemetery Tours
events.description:
Park City Museum will be giving tours of the Historic Glenwood Cemetery located at 401 Silver King Drive on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m. from June 30th to September 17th. We have also added three Saturday dates this year including July 25th, August 22nd, and September 5th from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m., and a few Monday evening tours including June 29th, July 6th, July 20th, August 10th, August 24th, and September 14th all starting at 5:30 p.m. 

Discover the history of the Cemetery and those residents who are buried there. Please register for a tour at www.parkcityhistory.org. Tours will be $15 per person. Please meet at the Glenwood Cemetery by the gate, bring a water bottle, wear sunscreen and comfortable walking shoes. Parking will be on Silver King Drive near the Cemetery. If you have any questions, please contact Diane Knispel education@parkcityhistory.org or call 435-574-9554....

events.title: Electric Avenue: The 80's MTV Experience
events.description:
We don’t get to repeat even one minute of our time here, but what if you could experience all the youthful energy and sonic celebration that was 1980s New Wave & Radio Pop?  Every sound. Every nuance. Every time.  That’s Electric Avenue. 

The Best 80s music hits performed the way you remember them.      

www.ElectricAvenueMusic.com

www.Facebook.com/ElectricAvenue

www.Instagram.com/ElectricAve80s

What started out as a one-off party gig has turned into a decade-long run, playing 100\+ live concerts each year across the US and abroad.

An Electric Avenue show is a high energy space that reminds people of a time when they came home from school, put a record on, and listened with open ears and an open heart; conjuring first dates, first cars, prom nights, weekend movies, and meeting friends at the mall, while finding a sense of fashion and an identity all their own. At an EA show, 80’s music is as alive and well today as it has ever been.

If authenticity is your thing, Electric Avenue meticulously and painstakingly recreates every nuance and flavor of your favorite 80’s songs. Delving into their personal stash of vintage synthesizers and rare signal processors, an Electric Avenue show is a true labor of love. In fact, Electric Avenue’s sound is so close to that of the original artists that in 2019 Warner Music issued a cease-and-desist for using original artists recordings on their social channels. After hearing the complete live recording of Electric Avenue’s performance, Warner Music issued an apology to the band. 

Electric Avenue has sailed the high seas with the The Walking Dead Cast, The Impractical Jokers, Joey Fatone, and Pitbull. In 2019, EA was hand-picked and flown to Asbury Park, NJ to provide the soundtrack for the red carpet launch of the Bruce Springsteen, Gurinder Chadha \(Bend it Like Beckham\), New Line Entertainment 80’s-centric film, Blinded By The Light. They have had the privilege of being direct support for 80’s legend Pat Benatar, and hav...

events.title: Fourth of July 5K
events.description:
Registration for Park City Ski & Snowboard’s annual 4th of July 5K is now open! This popular event has been an annual tradition since the early 1980’s and is a great way to support local young athletes as you kick off your 4th of July celebration!

Race registration is $30 for kids (11 and under) and $50 for adults (12 and over), and closes on July 3rd at 5:00 pm. Be sure to register early as we always sell out at our maximum of 1,000 runners. **THERE IS NO “DAY OF” REGISTRATION!!**

After you register, just pick up your race number and t-shirt at Cole Sport (1615 Park Avenue in Park City) between June 30-July 3 anytime between 10:00am-5:00pm each day. There is also a limited window to pick up these items on race day from 6:30-7:30am. The course map can be found here for your convenience. All proceeds benefit Park City Ski & Snowboard – the local club that provides world-class training and instruction to Park City athletes. Help support our Olympic hopefuls!...

We have also added three Saturday dates this year including July 25th, August 22nd, and September 5th from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m., and a few Monday evening tours including June 29th, July 6th, July 20th, August 10th, August 24th, and September 14th all starting at 5:30 p.m.

events.title: Main Street Walking Tour
events.description:
Put on your walking shoes and explore Park City’s Historic Main Street! Explore the architecture and stories of Park City’s unique history-from the days as a booming mine town to an emerging ski town. The Park City Museum’s popular Historic Main Street Public Walking Tours are Monday through Friday at 2 p.m. from June 29 to September 19. Meet at the Museum store. Cost is $15 per person. 

No tours on August 8 \(Kimball Arts Festival\) and September 7 \(Labor Day/Miners’ Day\). 

The tour is most appropriate for ages 13 and up. Please arrive 10 minutes early, wear comfortable walking shoes, bring water, and wear sun protection. The tour lasts about one hour and fifteen minutes. If you have general questions about the tour or want to schedule a private tour for twelve or more people, please contact Diane at 435-574-9554....

events.title: intro to embroidery
events.description: intro to embroidery Tuesday, July 7th | 6 PM | Our Lady of the Snows | $10 |  Sign-up Required In this embroidery class, students will learn foundational hand embroidery techniques and practice a variety of basic stitches using original patterns designed by the instructor. Participants will become familiar with tools and materials, including embroidery hoops, floss, needles, and fabric. Open to all skill levels, the class offers a relaxing and creative environment that encourages mindfulness and self-expression through fiber art. This event is for 12 and up. Ally Rocks is a Salt Lake City-based artist with a passion for embroidery, sewing, and graphic design. Inspired by reptiles, motorcycles, and travel, her work reflects a love of creativity and exploration. She holds a BA in Art History from New Mexico State University and an MA in Design from Joshibi University of Art and Design, where she studied as a recipient of the MEXT Scholarship Program. Her time living and studying in Japan continues to influence her artistic perspective and practice. Check out all her work at  Gaikotsudesign.com and on Instagram  @gaikotsu\_design ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility....

events.title: American Music in Provo: Presented by The A-Frame Concert Series — Park City Opera
events.description:
Tucked into Provo's Tree Streets neighborhood, The A-Frame is one of Utah's most unusual musical spaces: a midcentury-modern home built around an acoustically rich performance space. Part family home, part concert venue, it becomes the backdrop for an intimate evening of American art song and opera with Park City Opera.

Featuring nationally acclaimed singers from across the country, this performance offers a rare opportunity to experience operatic voices up close in a warm, 40-seat setting. Featuring selections from Aaron Copland's The Tender Land and other highlights of the American vocal tradition, join us for an evening of powerful, personal storytelling that captures the heart of the American spirit....

events.title: Breathwork + Soundbath
events.description:
A practice of Holotropic Breathwork, Meditation and Sound Bath to quiet the mind, open the heart & connect with ourselves & each other on a deeper level.

Breathwork is a healing practice and experience that we can all benefit from. Whether you are looking to reconnect to more joy & peace, de-stress from your work week, or to strengthen your connection to your intuition and creativity. The session will include a guided meditation and intention setting, introduction to holotropic breathwork and what to expect, a guided breathwork session followed by relaxing sound bath.

\*What to wear: comfortable clothing, long sleeves/layers encouraged

\* If you are pregnant or if you have epilepsy, please consult your primary care physician to see if breathwork is right for you before practicing.

Some benefits of breathwork include:

- Connect to your creativity

- Increase Self Love and appreciation

- Stress relief

- Increased Self Awareness

- Ease symptoms of Depression

- Ease symptoms of Anxiety

- Peace of mind

- Open the heart

- Increased Intuition

- Relaxation

- Release trauma and fear

- Move stuck energy and assist in processing emotions and feelings

10-Class Pass Rate: $11

Drop-In Rate: $22...

events.title: (Re)sounding: Art Exhibition
events.description: Sound is not simply something we hear, but something we inhabit, shape, and are shaped by. Through immersive audio installations, evocative visual translations of sound, and interactive artworks that invite participation, the artists in (Re)sounding explore sound as a dynamic subject and material. This exhibition is an invitation to listen more deeply—to feel the weight and resonance of sound, to dwell inside it, and to hear the world in new ways.

events.title: Opera on the Patio: Debussy & The Schumanns — Park City Opera
events.description:
On July 8, Opera on the Patio pairs the intimate, emotional ly direct songs of Robert Schumann and Clara Schumann with the luminous, color-rich world of Claude Debussy. These composers changed how music feels and moves - and we invite you to explore their works with us!

Back for its third season, Opera on the Patio is a free outdoor performance at the Park City Library. Bring a picnic, your family, and even your dog for an evening of beloved opera set against the backdrop of a summer sunset. Enjoy great music in a relaxed, welcoming setting \(no tickets required\).

Check back soon for more details, including the concert program, performer biographies, and more!...

events.title: Movement & Mindfulness Wed Trail Hike Series (Pt. 1)
events.description:
Breathe deeper. Move freer. Connect stronger.

Wild Women Tribe's fall Movement & Mindfulness Wed Weekly Trail Hike Series is back for the 4th season \(Wed 7/1 to Wed 8/5\)—six weeks of soul-fueled hikes, fresh air, and movement that awakens more than just your body.

It’s not just about the hike. It’s about the women walking beside you. The conversations that crack you open. The moments of presence & wonder. And the laughter that lingers long after the trail ends. Join our six-part hike series in Park City. We lace up and hit the dirt with a variety of modalities featuring local facilitators: from grounding somatic breathwork with Jilana McIntyre \(July 1\), a sacred blue lotus tea ceremony with Christina Sthair \(July 8\), to soul-soothing soundbath on the earth with Amanda Murphy \(July 29\), there's a little something for every woman seeking deep connection back to nature.

July 1: Jilana McIntyre \(somatic breathwork\)

July 8: Christina Sthair \(blue lotus tea ceremony\)

July 15: Sofia Mileti \(Foundation training\)

July 22: Renee Huang \(NLP style guided journal writing\)

July 29: Amanda Murphy \(soundbath meditation\)

Aug 5: Jai Nico \(Kundalini yoga activation\)

Can't make our kick off? There are five other dates filling up fast with community members looking to connect on the trails. We're hiking and moving weekly until Wed Aug 5. MORE DETAILS \>\>

Trail time is calling → SIGN UP NOW

DETAILS: wildwomentribe.net/workshops

Reserve your spot today: wildwomentribe.net/registration-2

VENMO: Sliding scale -- $40 to $55 suggested contribution \(@wildwomentribe -- last 4 digits 9583; do NOT select purchase protection or it'll deduct a fee you'll be asked to cover\)...

events.title: Volunteer Weed Pull
events.description:
Join the Swaner Preserve & EcoCenter in this outdoor project pulling invasive weeds! Invasive weeds crowd out native species and impair habitat. This is a great way to get onto areas of the Preserve that are not open to the public. Volunteers should be able to walk over uneven ground, bend over for extended periods, work outdoors in a variety of conditions, and comply with the safety guidelines. MEETING LOCATION TBD based on plant growth.

Tools and supplies are provided. Volunteers will learn invasive weed identification as well as methods for successful removal. Kids as young as 10 may accompany their parent or guardian for the duration of the project, but keep in mind the strenuous nature of the project. Unaccompanied minors must be 14+. If you have any questions regarding this, contact Rhea at rhea.cone@usu.edu....

events.title: Main Street Walking Tour
events.description:
Put on your walking shoes and explore Park City’s Historic Main Street! Explore the architecture and stories of Park City’s unique history-from the days as a booming mine town to an emerging ski town. The Park City Museum’s popular Historic Main Street Public Walking Tours are Monday through Friday at 2 p.m. from June 29 to September 19. Meet at the Museum store. Cost is $15 per person. 

No tours on August 8 \(Kimball Arts Festival\) and September 7 \(Labor Day/Miners’ Day\). 

The tour is most appropriate for ages 13 and up. Please arrive 10 minutes early, wear comfortable walking shoes, bring water, and wear sun protection. The tour lasts about one hour and fifteen minutes. If you have general questions about the tour or want to schedule a private tour for twelve or more people, please contact Diane at 435-574-9554....
events.location: Park City Museum, Park City, UT

events.title: Glenwood Cemetery Tours
events.description:
Park City Museum will be giving tours of the Historic Glenwood Cemetery located at 401 Silver King Drive on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m. from June 30th to September 17th. We have also added three Saturday dates this year including July 25th, August 22nd, and September 5th from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m., and a few Monday evening tours including June 29th, July 6th, July 20th, August 10th, August 24th, and September 14th all starting at 5:30 p.m. 

Discover the history of the Cemetery and those residents who are buried there. Please register for a tour at www.parkcityhistory.org. Tours will be $15 per person. Please meet at the Glenwood Cemetery by the gate, bring a water bottle, wear sunscreen and comfortable walking shoes. Parking will be on Silver King Drive near the Cemetery. If you have any questions, please contact Diane Knispel education@parkcityhistory.org or call 435-574-9554....
events.location: Glenwood Cemetery, Park City, UT

events.title: American Indian Services Native Market Days
events.description:
Native Market Days 2026 brings together a Native Market, Hoop Competition, and Indigenous Fashion Show for Native and non-Native communities in Park City. The event will take place July 10-11, 2026 at Canyons Village, with the Indigenous Art Market held in the Forum Tent, the Hoop Contest taking place at Canyons Village, and the Indigenous Fashion Show scheduled for Saturday, July 11 at 6:30 PM in the Grand Summit Ballroom.

The event celebrates Native artists, performers, designers, and community members through art, dance, fashion, storytelling, an d cultural engagement. The Native Market creates an opportunity for visitors to appreciate and support Native American artists and businesses, while the Hoop Competition honors Indigenous storytelling and tradition through dance. The Fashion Show will feature Indigenous designers and models sharing contemporary Native fashion on the runway in Park City.

Native Market Days is an opportunity to bring Native voices, creativity, and presence into one of Utah's most visible destination communities while supporting American Indian Services' broader mission of Native education, opportunity, and community connection....
events.location: Canyons Village at Park City, Park City, UT

events.title: Park City Film Presents: Power Ballad
events.description:
When Rick (Paul Rudd), a past-his-prime wedding singer, meets fading boy-band star Danny (Nick Jonas) during a gig, the two bond over music and a late-night jam session. But when Danny turns one of Rick’s songs into the hit that reignites his career, Rick sets out to reclaim the recognition he believes he deserves. From writer-director John Carney (SING STREET, ONCE), “Power Ballad” is a feel-good story about music, self-respect, friendship, and the price of ambition.

$8 for general admission, $7 for students and seniors, and $6 for members of Park City Film.

events.title: Breathwork + Soundbath
events.description:
A practice of Holotropic Breathwork, Meditation and Sound Bath to quiet the mind, open the heart & connect with ourselves & each other on a deeper level.

Breathwork is a healing practice and experience that we can all benefit from. Whether you are looking to reconnect to more joy & peace, de-stress from your work week, or to strengthen your connection to your intuition and creativity. The session will include a guided meditation and intention setting, introduction to holotropic breathwork and what to expect, a guided breathwork session followed by relaxing sound bath.

\*What to wear: comfortable clothing, long sleeves/layers encouraged

\* If you are pregnant or if you have epilepsy, please consult your primary care physician to see if breathwork is right for you before practicing.

Some benefits of breathwork include:

- Connect to your creativity

- Increase Self Love and appreciation

- Stress relief

- Increased Self Awareness

- Ease symptoms of Depression

- Ease symptoms of Anxiety

- Peace of mind

- Open the heart

- Increased Intuition

- Relaxation

- Release trauma and fear

- Move stuck energy and assist in processing emotions and feelings

10-Class Pass Rate: $11

Drop-In Rate: $22...

events.title: Movement & Mindfulness Wed Trail Hike Series (Pt. 1)
events.description:
Breathe deeper. Move freer. Connect stronger.

Wild Women Tribe's fall Movement & Mindfulness Wed Weekly Trail Hike Series is back for the 4th season \(Wed 7/1 to Wed 8/5\)—six weeks of soul-fueled hikes, fresh air, and movement that awakens more than just your body.

It’s not just about the hike. It’s about the women walking beside you. The conversations that crack you open. The moments of presence & wonder. And the laughter that lingers long after the trail ends. Join our six-part hike series in Park City. We lace up and hit the dirt with a variety of modalities featuring local facilitators: from grounding somatic breathwork with Jilana McIntyre \(July 1\), a sacred blue lotus tea ceremony with Christina Sthair \(July 8\), to soul-soothing soundbath on the earth with Amanda Murphy \(July 29\), there's a little something for every woman seeking deep connection back to nature.

July 1: Jilana McIntyre \(somatic breathwork\)

July 8: Christina Sthair \(blue lotus tea ceremony\)

July 15: Sofia Mileti \(Foundation training\)

July 22: Renee Huang \(NLP style guided journal writing\)

July 29: Amanda Murphy \(soundbath meditation\)

Aug 5: Jai Nico \(Kundalini yoga activation\)

Can't make our kick off? There are five other dates filling up fast with community members looking to connect on the trails. We're hiking and moving weekly until Wed Aug 5. MORE DETAILS \>\>

Trail time is calling → SIGN UP NOW

DETAILS: wildwomentribe.net/workshops

Reserve your spot today: wildwomentribe.net/registration-2

VENMO: Sliding scale -- $40 to $55 suggested contribution \(@wildwomentribe -- last 4 digits 9583; do NOT select purchase protection or it'll deduct a fee you'll be asked to cover\)...

events.title: Sisters of the Holy Cross lecture part 2 by David Nicholas & Stuart Stanek
events.description:
Park City Museum will host a lecture called *Sisters of the Holy Cross Part 2* by David Nicholas and Stuart Stanek on Wednesday, July 15, 2026 from 5-6 p.m. held at the Education and Collections Center located at 2079 Sidewinder Drive.  More information can be found at www.parkcityhistory.org.  This presentation follows their November 12, 2025 lecture on the Sisters of the Holy Cross and the Order’s 146 years of service to underserved communities in Utah and Park City.

The evening’s discussion will focus on the Sisters’ controversial efforts between approximately 1980 and 1994 to expand access to health care in a community where medical services were otherwise limited to Salt Lake City. Topics will include their management of the Park City Ambulance Corps, plans to establish a hospital in Kimball Junction, and their heroic response to the HIV/AIDS epidemic at a time when other regional medical institutions failed to address the growing crisis.

David Nicholas serves on the Board of the Park City Museum, is interested in local history, and frequently contributes to the *Way We Were* series. Also, he conducts periodic lectures and historic hikes. His particular interests include but are not limited to Park City’s transportation history and local plane crashes.

 Stuart Stanek is a forty-year broadcasting veteran.  He managed one hundred and twenty-five radio stations in twenty-five different markets across the country. He served as a general manager and a regional President for iHeart Radio which is the nation’s largest broadcaster. He was involved in all facets of the business including sales, programming, operations, and marketing.  He is married to Ann Crooks and they have lived in Park City for thirty-five years....
events.location: Park City Museum, Park City, UT

events.title: Main Street Walking Tour
events.description:
Put on your walking shoes and explore Park City’s Historic Main Street! Explore the architecture and stories of Park City’s unique history-from the days as a booming mine town to an emerging ski town. The Park City Museum’s popular Historic Main Street Public Walking Tours are Monday through Friday at 2 p.m. from June 29 to September 19. Meet at the Museum store. Cost is $15 per person. 

No tours on August 8 \(Kimball Arts Festival\) and September 7 \(Labor Day/Miners’ Day\). 

The tour is most appropriate for ages 13 and up. Please arrive 10 minutes early, wear comfortable walking shoes, bring water, and wear sun protection. The tour lasts about one hour and fifteen minutes. If you have general questions about the tour or want to schedule a private tour for twelve or more people, please contact Diane at 435-574-9554....
events.location: Park City Museum, Park City, UT

events.description:
Park City Museum will be giving tours of the Historic Glenwood Cemetery located at 401 Silver King Drive on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m. from June 30th to September 17th. We have also added three Saturday dates this year including July 25th, August 22nd, and September 5th from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m., and a few Monday evening tours including June 29th, July 6th, July 20th, August 10th, August 24th, and September 14th all starting at 5:30 p.m. 

Discover the history of the Cemetery and those residents who are buried there. Please register for a tour at www.parkcityhistory.org. Tours will be $15 per person. Please meet at the Glenwood Cemetery by the gate, bring a water bottle, wear sunscreen and comfortable walking shoes. Parking will be on Silver King Drive near the Cemetery. If you have any questions, please contact Diane Knispel education@parkcityhistory.org or call 435-574-9554....

events.title: Raising Voices En Pointe - Free Screening + Q&A
events.description: RAISING VOICES EN POINTE - Free film screening \+ Q&A Ballet lovers hold deep reverence for classics like The Nutcracker and La Bayadère, and for decades many fans overlooked the racist Asian caricatures embedded in some of these productions. When ballet dancers Phil Chan \(resident choreographer at the Oakland Ballet Company\) and Georgina Pazcoguin \(first female AAPI soloist at the New York City Ballet and recent Ballet West guest artist\) launched the Final Bow for Yellow Face movement, they were met with pushback from traditionalists who accused them of trying to change history. Post-film discussion and reception with Ballet West Artistic Director Adam Sklute and Phil Chan, co-founder of Final Bow for Yellowface. Part of the Ballet West Summit Festival July 15-17....

events.title: 2026 Deer Valley Music Festival: Beethoven's Ninth
events.description: If there's one classical masterpiece everyone should experience live, it's Beethoven's Symphony No. 9. From its mysterious opening to its thrilling finale, the piece builds toward a powerful celebration of humanity and hope with the "Ode to Joy," as the orchestra, chorus, and soloists unite in one of the most recognizable melodies ever written. Now imagine hearing it beneath the open skies and surrounded by the Wasatch Mountains, performed by the extraordinary musicians of the Utah Symphony, the Utah Symphony Chorus, and vocal soloists under the baton of our Music Director Designate Markus Poschner-an internationally acclaimed Beethoven interpreter, making his first appearance at our summer home. Beethoven's Ninth is a monumental work and an unforgettable way to open our summer season....

events.description: Ballet West presents Ballet West at the Summit July 17, 2026 - 7 PM Canyons Village Amphitheater 4000 Canyons Resort Drive Park City, UT 84098 Single tickets on sale: 4/8/2026 Doors open 5:30 PM Ballet West Principal Dancers Katlyn Addison, Jenna Rae Herrera, Hadriel Diniz, and David Huffmire performing three lively works: Q.V.C. by Lou Christensen, L'air d'Esprit by Gerard Aripino, and After the Rain Pas de Deux by Christopher Wheeldon. Includes an artÉmotion Choreographic Workshop performance featuring students from the Frederick Quinney Lawson Ballet West Academy performing world premieres by internationally renowned choreographers Phil Chan, Ingrid Silva, Garrett Smith, and Rex Tilton. Run time: approximately 2 hours including intermission \(subject to change\) Recommended for ages 8 and up. No babes in arms. All patrons must have a ticket regardless of age. Portable chairs of any height allowed, there is a designated section for low-height \(9″ or less from ground\) and standard-height chairs. Blankets and picnic baskets/charcuterie/food are welcome \(no alcoholic beverages allowed\), no glass containers, beverages must be in factory-sealed plastic containers 32oz or less, empty reuseable bottles allowed 32oz or less and water stations available. All bags may be subject to search. Free parking available but paid hotel parking also available. ADA seating available. events.title: Gorgeous Gourds
events.description:
Psychedelic Funk band Gorgeous Gourds combine smooth harmonies, deep tasty grooves, and original songwriting.

The Gorgeous Gourd's just released album \(2/22/22\) "Peace and Love" reminds us why we go see and hear live music. The band has evolved to a rock solid synergistic group of accomplished musicians. Their powerful singing and songwriting is evident on their new CD, with 14 worthy cuts combining an eclectic combination of psychedelic funk, folk, tasty jam grooves, smooth harmonies and soulful ballads. Fred, Lucas, and Sarah all share the artful original songwriting. Iconic KRCL DJ John Florence commented on the new album, "Not a stinker in the bunch!”

Formed in Salt Lake City, Utah in 2015, founding members are Fred Hayes \(bass, vocals\), and the strong and steady Bob Smith \(drums\). Lucas Jones \(lead guitar,vocals\) provides sublime and powerful guitar licks and vocals, together with the guitar of Jacob Dahlberg and the dazzling vocals of Heather McCartin. 

Whether you enjoy easing into a bluesy flow ride, or getting all the kinks out on the dance floor, the Gorgeous Gourds is the party you'll keep coming back to. The title of their new album "Peace and Love" is a message they spread with unbridled energy and enthusiasm.

Like they say, "You're Gorgeous, we're the Gourds. Together we are Gorgeous Gourds."

GorgeousGourds.net...

events.description:
Deeply rooted in the American spirit, Copland's The Tender Land portrays one young woman's quiet but radical act of choosing her own future. Set against the open landscapes of the American West, The Tender Land is a coming-of-age story about longing, independence, and resilience in the face of hardship.

As Laurie stands on the brink of her high school graduation, the arrival of a handsome outsider awakens dreams of a life beyond her family's farm. When hope, love, and disappointment collide, she must decide whether to remain within the life she knows or step boldly into the unknown.

events.title: wasatch wildflower festival
events.description: Sunday, July 19th | 9am-2pm | Alta Ski Area | Free |  Registration is Required Held annually, the  Wasatch Wildflower Festival is a multi-day event hosted at the four Cottonwood Canyons ski areas to celebrate the beauty and diversity of wildflowers in the Wasatch Mountains. The 2026 Festival will be held on the following dates and locations: July 11 - Brighton Ski Resort July 12 - Solitude Mountain Resort July 18 - Snowbird Ski Resort July 19 - Alta Ski Area The Festival begins at 9 am each day, and the last guided walk leaves at 1 pm. The event is free, but registration is required.  Registration is now open! Founded in 1996 by a group of Alta locals and Alta Community Enrichment \(one of their first events\), the Festival is a special opportunity to educate visitors on how to ensure the protection of this important and unique natural resource. The Wasatch Wildflower Festival's guided walks and activities are designed to be fun, family-friendly, non-commercial, free of charge, and open to all interests and ability levels. Volunteers share their knowledge and enthusiasm for an amazing variety of species on a variety of beautiful walks. The tours also reinforce careful stewardship and awareness of the ecology of the watershed environment. Come enjoy multiple days of wildflower walks, kids' crafts, music, food, and a healthy dose of the beautiful Cottonwood Canyons. The 2025 Festival is cohosted with the Brighton Institute at Brighton and Solitude. Snowbird Sustainability, and Alta Community Enrichment and Alta Environmental Center, are cohosting at Snowbird and Alta, respectively....
events.location: Alta Ski Area, Alta, UT

events.title: Volunteer Dig Day July 19th - Anchor Trees
events.description: Greetings Volunteers! We're looking forward to working with you and would like to share details about our upcoming dig day. We'll be meeting at the private parking area directly west of the Bonanza Flat parking lot at 8:30 am \(no fee to park\). We'll then head out to do dirt work on the new hiking trail connecting 9k and Mid Mountain. If you're a mountain biker wondering why you should be doing work on a hike-only trail, please remember that these options alleviate traffic and help to optimize bike-only options throughout the system.  We strive to make our volunteer days as productive and fulfilling as possible. This project presents an excellent opportunity to create a unique legacy trail that will benefit you and the community for years to come! Timeframe: 8:30-8:45 Meet at the trailhead 8:45 Head to worksite 12:00 Return to trailhead Waivers are required every year, for each participant, and MUST be filled out beforehand: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdGpEehj4t8X4AkxwyseMgjIzErC6p2WDGm1lWhIxUdKM6DiQ/viewform?vc=0&c=0&w=1&flr=0 For your comfort and safety, please come prepared with the following: Water Sun Protection Gloves Snacks Closed-Toe Footwear Dig-day events are RSVP only. Please be sure to mark "Going" to the Facebook event so that we can accommodate you. If you have any questions, please contact Ian Hartley : Ian@mountaintrails.org...

events.title: Breathwork + Soundbath
events.description:
A practice of Holotropic Breathwork, Meditation and Sound Bath to quiet the mind, open the heart & connect with ourselves & each other on a deeper level.

Breathwork is a healing practice and experience that we can all benefit from. Whether you are looking to reconnect to more joy & peace, de-stress from your work week, or to strengthen your connection to your intuition and creativity. The session will include a guided meditation and intention setting, introduction to holotropic breathwork and what to expect, a guided breathwork session followed by relaxing sound bath.

\*What to wear: comfortable clothing, long sleeves/layers encouraged

\* If you are pregnant or if you have epilepsy, please consult your primary care physician to see if breathwork is right for you before practicing.

Some benefits of breathwork include:

- Connect to your creativity

- Increase Self Love and appreciation

- Stress relief

- Increased Self Awareness

- Ease symptoms of Depression

- Ease symptoms of Anxiety

- Peace of mind

- Open the heart

- Increased Intuition

- Relaxation

- Release trauma and fear

- Move stuck energy and assist in processing emotions and feelings

10-Class Pass Rate: $11

Drop-In Rate: $22...

events.title: Mozart's "Jupiter"
events.description: Utah Symphony Music Director Designate Markus Poschner leads an all-Mozart program starring Concertmaster Madeline Adkins in Mozart's second violin concerto. Written when he was just 18, the concerto showcases effortless virtuosity with the elegance and clarity that would continue to define his voice throughout his career. Framing the concerto are Mozart's two final symphonies—written just weeks apart—revealing the fascinating journey of the composer&\#03's musical maturation and his unmistakable brilliance.One of Mozart's most recognizable works, his Symphony No. 40 uses a minor key—a rare choice for the composer—to build emotional intensity and dark lyricism. In contrast, the program concludes with the joyful Symphony No. 41, whose grandeur and musical tour de force ultimately earned it the nickname “Jupiter,” honoring the Roman god....
events.location: St. Mary's Church, Park City, UT

events.title: Movement & Mindfulness Wed Trail Hike Series (Pt. 1)
events.description:
Breathe deeper. Move freer. Connect stronger.

Wild Women Tribe's fall Movement & Mindfulness Wed Weekly Trail Hike Series is back for the 4th season \(Wed 7/1 to Wed 8/5\)—six weeks of soul-fueled hikes, fresh air, and movement that awakens more than just your body.

It’s not just about the hike. It’s about the women walking beside you. The conversations that crack you open. The moments of presence & wonder. And the laughter that lingers long after the trail ends. Join our six-part hike series in Park City. We lace up and hit the dirt with a variety of modalities featuring local facilitators: from grounding somatic breathwork with Jilana McIntyre \(July 1\), a sacred blue lotus tea ceremony with Christina Sthair \(July 8\), to soul-soothing soundbath on the earth with Amanda Murphy \(July 29\), there's a little something for every woman seeking deep connection back to nature.

July 1: Jilana McIntyre \(somatic breathwork\)

July 8: Christina Sthair \(blue lotus tea ceremony\)

July 15: Sofia Mileti \(Foundation training\)

July 22: Renee Huang \(NLP style guided journal writing\)

July 29: Amanda Murphy \(soundbath meditation\)

Aug 5: Jai Nico \(Kundalini yoga activation\)

Can't make our kick off? There are five other dates filling up fast with community members looking to connect on the trails. We're hiking and moving weekly until Wed Aug 5. MORE DETAILS \>\>

Trail time is calling → SIGN UP NOW

DETAILS: wildwomentribe.net/workshops

Reserve your spot today: wildwomentribe.net/registration-2

VENMO: Sliding scale -- $40 to $55 suggested contribution \(@wildwomentribe -- last 4 digits 9583; do NOT select purchase protection or it'll deduct a fee you'll be asked to cover\)...

events.title: Volunteer Weed Pull
events.description:
Join the Swaner Preserve & EcoCenter in this outdoor project pulling invasive weeds! Invasive weeds crowd out native species and impair habitat. This is a great way to get onto areas of the Preserve that are not open to the public. Volunteers should be able to walk over uneven ground, bend over for extended periods, work outdoors in a variety of conditions, and comply with the safety guidelines. MEETING LOCATION TBD based on plant growth.

Tools and supplies are provided. Volunteers will learn invasive weed identification as well as methods for successful removal. Kids as young as 10 may accompany their parent or guardian for the duration of the project, but keep in mind the strenuous nature of the project. Unaccompanied minors must be 14+. If you have any questions regarding this, contact Rhea at rhea.cone@usu.edu....

events.title: Pendry Plaza
events.description:
Raised in a family full of musicians, music is a natural calling for Salt Lake City based artist Angie Petty. Taking influence from artists like Amy Winehouse, India Arie and Moonchild, Angie's unique sound sparkles in a pop/jazz world with a smoothness that will leave you wanting more.

 

Packed with an arsenal full of tools, Petty is hands on when it comes to producing, arranging, writing, performing and recording her music. These many talents have lended opportunities in sharing stages with the likes of Grammy Award Winning artists Jon Batiste, Melvin Jones, Philip Lassiter and Elliot Yamin.

 

In 2021, she released her debut album “Timing,” landing her song “Love Letter” on multiple Spotify editorial placements. Angie's mission is to share her life experiences through simple yet vulnerable lyrics to remind people they are never alone. A hidden gem in the making, Angie Petty is a rising star to watch, with multiple new releases and tour dates coming in 2024....

Join us for an incredible night of good vibes and heavy grooves as we bring our unique style of high desert reggae, rock, and groove music to Park City!

The Last Light is a reggae based groove collective composed of 7 artists who call the Southern Utah Desert home. With a lineup of players each with their own deep musical resumes and variety of styles to bring to the table, they've set out to create a brand new sound blending reggae, rock, hip-hop, & funk together into an electric sound. 

They Released their Debut EP, "Music Is The Medicine" in 2023 and have performed shows in Utah, Nevada, California, & Florida in the last 4 years. Their newest single “I’ll Be Ok” was released 2/27/26.

Always with the crowd in mind, they bring all of their energy to their performances with one singular goal - to get you on your feet, out of your mind, and into the music. Bringing full spectrum vibes that mirror a complete human experience - The Last Light's three vocalists, Charlie, Kaleb, and Eli will bring you lyrics that make you feel, lyrics that make you laugh, and together they bring thick grooves that make you dance....

events.title: 80s Weekend with 38 Special
events.description:
38 Special – Live in Concert
Bring on the anthems. 38 Special delivers high-energy, Southern-fried rock that defined an era and still packs the house decades later. Known for blending catchy hooks with driving guitar riffs, the band has become a staple of American rock radio and live performance.
With iconic hits like “Hold On Loosely,” “Caught Up in You,” and “Rockin’ Into the Night,” 38 Special has built a legacy of feel-good, sing-along songs that cross generations. Their live shows are tight, powerful, and packed with the kind of rock-and-roll energy that turns a concert into a shared memory.
Whether you grew up with these songs or discovered them later, this is a show made for fans of classic rock, summer nights, and timeless music that still hits just right.
 
Fox Brothers Band
Get ready for an unforgettable night of high-energy entertainment with the Fox Brothers Band, one of Utah’s most sought-after live music acts. Blending the best of Country, Rock, and Pop, the band delivers a dynamic performance packed with crowd favorites, modern hits, and timeless classics that keep audiences singing and dancing all night long.
Known for their infectious energy, exceptional musicianship, and engaging stage presence, the Fox Brothers Band has built a reputation for creating memorable live experiences for audiences of all ages. events.description:
Adaptation of the Broadway musical & DreamWorks film. This is a one-of-a-kind, hilarious fairy tale in which curses are reversed; monsters get the girls, donkeys & dragons find love, & princesses are beautiful in all shapes & sizes.

Presented by The Egyptian YouTheatre and performed by an All Youth Cast! Fri 7pm, Sat 2pm & 7pm. All ticket sales support our successful YouTheatre program. Pharaohs, use your promo code for the best seats.

The Egyptian YouTheatre presents Shrek Jr. on the Main Stage.

Presented by Egyptian YouTheatre and performed by an all-youth cast! Once upon a time, in a faraway swamp, there lived an ogre named Shrek. One day, Shrek finds his swamp invaded by banished fairytale misfits who have been cast off by Lord Farquaad, a tiny terror with big ambitions. When Shrek sets off with a wisecracking donkey to confront Farquaad, he's handed a task - if he rescues feisty princess Fiona, his swamp will be righted. Shrek tries to win Fiona's love and vanquish Lord Farquaad, but a fairytale wouldn't be complete without a few twists and turns along the way.

events.title: Stravinsky's Firebird Suite & String Trio "Time for Three
events.description:
Stravinsky's Firebird Suite takes flight in this spectacular program led by Utah Symphony Music Director Designate Markus Poschner.

First, GRAMMY®-winning trio Time for Three-known for their genre-defying blend of classical, Americana, and contemporary styles- takes the stage for Kevin Puts' Contact, the winner of a GRAMMY® for Best Contemporary Classical Composition. Written specifically for the group by the Pulitzer Prize-winning composer, the work explores themes of human connection and highlights the trio's dynamic, longtime partnership with the Utah Symphony.

Then, hear one of the most thrilling pieces ever written echoing through the Wasatch Mountains as the orchestra takes you on a journey from the explosive "Infernal Dance" to the powerful finale of Stravinsky's Firebird.

At its heart is the world’s first fully adaptive trail network—built to prove that everyone belongs outside. Visually stunning and emotionally powerful, “Best Day Ever” challenges perceptions of disability and celebrates community. Post-film Q&A with Greg Durso. Reception to follow in Community Room. events.title: Overland Cookery Weekend
events.description:
Experience the flavors of the mountains at Silver Lake, where open-fire cooking meets alpine views. Led by Overland Cookery's expedition chefs, this weekend draws inspiration from cooking in remote environments. Open fire techniques and thoughtful preparation create an experience that feels both elevated and grounded. Set on Silver Lake Lawn, it is an easy, social setting to spend the afternoon, where food, fire, an d the outdoors come together.

Throughout the lawn, discover standout culinary moments, from Chef Peter Menteer's showstopping eight-foot paella filled with fresh seafood and local meats to Chef Sam Phelan's plank salmon, slow-cooked over the fire. Pair each bite with craft pours from Utah breweries and live music.

Admission is free with lift access from Snow Park. Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate....

events.title: 80s Weekend with Sawyer Brown
events.description:
“We don’t have a lot of love songs. We have life songs.” – Mark Miller
For more than 40 years, Sawyer Brown has delivered high-energy, blue-collar country-rock that keeps audiences on their feet. From winning Star Search to sharing stages with legends like Kenny Rogers and Dolly Parton, they’ve built a career defined by nonstop touring and unforgettable live shows.
With 51 charted singles—including the #1 hit “Some Girls Do”—and over 6,000 performances worldwide, Sawyer Brown continues to bring the same grassroots spirit and electrifying presence that made them a household name.
Skylar Geer
Experience an evening of authentic country music with Utah singer-songwriter Skylar Geer, a talented performer known for his rich vocals, heartfelt songwriting, and energetic live shows. Blending traditional country roots with Southern rock influences, Skylar creates a sound that is both timeless and fresh, connecting with audiences through stories of life, love, and the American West.
After spending several years in Nashville honing his craft as a songwriter and performer, Skylar returned to Utah and has become a favorite on stages throughout the Mountain West. Backed by a talented band featuring fiddle, guitars, bass, and drums, his performances seamlessly mix original music with crowd-favorite country classics and modern hits, all delivered with his signature style.
